title: lippia campestris
intro: Lippia campestris, also known as wild marsh marigold or field lippia, is a perennial herb belonging to the verbena family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Lippia campestris include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. Lippia campestris can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is often used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of the world.
